essence: Installs, tests, and maintains fire‑alarm systems to ensure buildings meet safety standards and remain protected.

identity.title: Fire Alarm Technician
identity.summary: Installs, inspects, and repairs fire‑alarm systems, ensuring they function correctly and comply with fire‑safety regulations.

salary.notes: Specialists in complex commercial systems, addressable networks, or multi‑site maintenance can exceed £45k-£55k+.

qualifications: Training in fire‑alarm installation and maintenance
Knowledge of BS 5839 and fire‑safety regulations
Ability to test, fault‑find, and certify alarm systems
Understanding of wiring, control panels, and detection devices

lifestyle.description: Fire alarm technicians work across homes, businesses, and public buildings installing and maintaining fire‑alarm systems. The role involves wiring, testing, fault‑finding, and ensuring compliance with safety standards. It suits individuals who enjoy technical work, problem‑solving, and contributing to building safety.

habits: Follows strict safety and wiring standards
Keeps accurate maintenance and inspection records
